proved: The Rust adapter posts to the exact /v1/responses route with bearer authentication.
The adapter forces store=false and stream=false and sends neither previous_response_id nor prompt_cache_key.
A successful response is normalized into the provider-neutral response model and attributed to xAI.
The response must contain a boolean x-zero-data-retention header and the parsed value is surfaced in the typed result.
A false retention header is kept distinct from true, so standard response retention is not represented as enterprise zero-data retention.
Disclosure denial, provider errors, timeout, and cancellation produce distinct sanitized failures.
xAI credentials use a vault account namespace distinct from every other provider.
All assertions ran against an in-process loopback fake server without a real key or paid request.
notProved: Tool calls, encrypted reasoning, usage accounting, or cost accounting.
The live service's retention behavior, enterprise policy, or zero-data-retention eligibility.
Product UI, Tauri commands, or credential-entry wiring.
A live xAI request or model-output quality.
